summary: Renamed the generic fighter source/header pair `ftdata3.c/.h` to `ftCo_Init.c/.h` and moved them under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/`. Updated `obj_files.mk` so the build uses `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Init.c.o` instead of the old `ftdata3.c.o` path. The file contents were unchanged according to the 100% similarity rename.
what_changed: Replaced the `TEXT_O_FILES` entry in `obj_files.mk` from `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftdata3.c.o` to `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Init.c.o`.
No source contents were changed in the diff excerpt; this was a pure path/name cleanup.
smart_moves: Used a 100% similarity rename, keeping the change narrowly scoped and easy to review.
Updated the object file list alongside the source move, preserving build inclusion after the path change.
Moved a previously generic `ftdata3` file into the more descriptive `ft/chara/ftCommon` hierarchy, aligning the file location with the `ftCo_` common-fighter naming pattern.
decomp_lessons: When a generic placeholder file name gains a more specific identity, perform the rename without source edits when possible so reviewers and future archaeology can distinguish naming cleanup from matching changes.
File moves in this repo often require matching updates in `obj_files.mk`; a source rename alone is not enough if the object list still points at the old path.
Common fighter code should prefer descriptive `ftCo_*` naming and placement under `ft/chara/ftCommon` when evidence supports that classification.
naming_conventions: `ftdata3` was replaced by `ftCo_Init`, indicating a move away from anonymous data/file numbering toward subsystem-specific fighter-common names.
`ftCo_` appears to be the prefix used for common fighter code.
The path `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/` is the chosen home for this common fighter initialization file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code matching tactic was shown; the rename had 100% file similarity and only adjusted the build object path.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that edit `ftCo_Init.c` or `ftCo_Init.h` for the actual function names and initialization responsibilities.
Check whether includes or references to `ftdata3.h` were updated in adjacent PRs, since this slice only shows the file rename and object list update.
Search for other numbered `ftdata*` files that were later renamed into `ftCo_*` or moved under `ft/chara/ftCommon`.
